---
title: "ECON 105: Introduction to Mathematical Economics"

institution: "UCLA"
term: "Fall 2025"
role: "Instructor"
order: 1

description: >
  Undergraduate statistics course covering probability, random variables,
  estimation, confidence intervals, hypothesis testing, and applied data analysis.

syllabus: "../../files/teaching/econ105-syllabus.pdf"
---

## Course overview

This course covers the essential mathematical and computing skills needed to study modern economics. We will review both differential and integral calculus (first derivatives, partial derivatives, optimization, integration), as well as the basics of Excel and R (a statistical programming language).

## Topics

- Probability and conditional probability
- Discrete and continuous random variables
- Expected value and variance
- Binomial, Poisson, uniform, and normal distributions
- Central limit theorem
- Confidence intervals
- Hypothesis testing
- Introductory data analysis in Excel and R

## Materials

- [Syllabus](../../files/teaching/econ105-syllabus.pdf)
- [Lecture slides](#)
- [Practice problems](#)
- [Data exercises](#)
